ranking.name: UL2/T5 Encoder-Decoder + Encoder JEPA Auxiliary
strengths: Keeps token CE as the generation contract.
Gives the encoder a direct semantic prediction task.
Allows staged or mixed denoising objectives.
JEPA components are train-time only and can be removed at inference.
risks: Too much latent loss can hurt decoder fluency.
EMA target and predictor add training compute.
Needs collapse monitoring, not just loss curves.

ranking.name: Plain T5 Span-Corruption + Encoder JEPA Auxiliary
strengths: Easy to implement and compare against a normal T5 baseline.
Strong fit for summarization, translation, QA, and instruction-to-output tasks.
risks: May undertrain causal/prefix behavior unless added explicitly.

ranking.name: BART Denoising + Encoder JEPA Auxiliary
strengths: Denoising aligns naturally with masked-context JEPA.
Strong bidirectional encoder plus generation decoder.
risks: Less unified than UL2 for mixing multiple language modeling modes.
Noising policy can dominate results, making JEPA contribution harder to isolate.

ranking.name: Decoder-Only SLM + Latent Self-Distillation Head
strengths: Matches common inference stacks for chat/completion.
risks: Causal attention weakens the bidirectional context that makes JEPA/data2vec targets rich.
Auxiliary latent prediction can become redundant with next-token prediction.

ranking.name: Pure Text JEPA Encoder, Decoder Added Later
strengths: Useful if the product is embeddings, retrieval, or classification.
risks: Later decoder attachment can waste pretraining or require expensive alignment.

sources.title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
sources.core_claim: A text-to-text encoder-decoder can use one maximum-likelihood training interface across many NLP tasks.
sources.relevance: Base architecture for small generative encoder-decoder language models.

sources.title: BART: Denoising Sequence-to-Sequence Pre-training for Natural Language Generation, Translation, and Comprehension
sources.core_claim: Corrupt text, then train an encoder-decoder to reconstruct it; the bidirectional encoder and left-to-right decoder serve both comprehension and generation.
sources.relevance: Shows why denoising seq2seq remains the generation contract that JEPA should not replace.

sources.title: UL2: Unifying Language Learning Paradigms
sources.core_claim: Mixture-of-Denoisers combines pretraining paradigms and improves the Pareto frontier versus T5- and GPT-like objectives.
sources.relevance: Best template for mixing CE denoising, prefix-LM behavior, and auxiliary latent learning.

sources.title: data2vec: A General Framework for Self-supervised Learning in Speech, Vision and Language
sources.core_claim: Predict contextualized latent representations of the full input from a masked view, including for NLP.
sources.relevance: Closest direct precedent for text JEPA-style latent target prediction.

sources.title: Efficient Self-supervised Learning with Contextualized Target Representations for Vision, Speech and Language
sources.core_claim: Skipping masked-token encoding, using a fast decoder, and amortizing teacher computation can make latent target learning much cheaper.
sources.relevance: Supports a small predictor and careful compute accounting for 0.2B models.

sources.title: Self-Supervised Learning from Images with a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture
sources.core_claim: Predict target-block representations from context-block representations, with EMA target encoder and a predictor.
sources.relevance: Provides the JEPA design pattern: context encoder, target encoder, predictor, latent loss.

sources.title: VICReg: Variance-Invariance-Covariance Regularization for Self-Supervised Learning
sources.core_claim: Variance and covariance terms can explicitly prevent constant-representation collapse.
sources.relevance: Useful as diagnostics or optional regularizers for JEPA hidden states.

sources.title: Barlow Twins: Self-Supervised Learning via Redundancy Reduction
sources.core_claim: Driving cross-correlation toward identity encourages invariance while reducing redundant embedding dimensions.
sources.relevance: Mathematical guardrail for representation quality and collapse checks.

sources.title: SpacTor-T5: Pre-training T5 Models with Span Corruption and Replaced Token Detection
sources.core_claim: A staged hybrid objective can improve T5 pretraining efficiency, with reported reductions in iterations and FLOPs.
sources.relevance: Supports staged auxiliary objectives instead of applying all losses equally forever.

sources.title: Var-JEPA: A Variational Formulation of the Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture
sources.core_claim: JEPA can be interpreted as a deterministic specialization of a latent-variable variational objective.
sources.relevance: Gives a mathematical bridge between predictive latent learning and generative modeling theory.
